message:
feat(planner,core): populate AccessPathProbe + thread it through index directive codegen

Follow-up to 068ce10a which landed the AccessPathProbe shape-only
wiring. Two complementary changes: the planner now actually
populates `AccessPath::probe` at the end of best-path selection,
and the connection seam consumes that probe for
IndexScanEquality/IndexScanRange directive construction instead of
re-parsing the WHERE clause.

crates/fsqlite-planner/src/lib.rs
- AccessPathProbe variants now box their `Expr` payloads:
    RowidEquality { target: Box<Expr> }
    Equality   { column: String, target: Box<Expr> }
    Range      { column, lower: Option<(Box<Expr>, bool)>,
                         upper: Option<(Box<Expr>, bool)> }
  Keeps the AccessPath struct small (it's held per-candidate during
  cost comparisons) and matches the boxed-Expr convention the AST
  already uses for recursive positions.
- New private `extract_comparison_operand(expr)` helper — given a
  BinaryOp Expr, return the non-column side so probes can cache
  "the RHS of col = <rhs>" without reparsing.
- New private `extract_access_path_probe(best, indexes, where_terms)`
  dispatches on AccessPathKind:
    FullTableScan  -> None (no probe to record)
    RowidLookup    -> RowidEquality { target } from the terms that
                      drove the rowid choice
    IndexScanEquality -> Equality { column, target } matching the
                      leading index column
    IndexScanRange -> Range { column, lower, upper } reading both
                      bounds out of the range WhereTerms
  Index name matching is `eq_ignore_ascii_case` (matches the rest
  of the planner's casefolding policy for identifiers).
- `best_access_path_internal` calls
  `extract_access_path_probe(&best, indexes, where_terms)` once at
  the tail and stores the result on `best.probe`. All earlier
  AccessPath construction sites still init `probe: None` so
  intermediate candidates don't pay for extraction until we know
  which path won.

crates/fsqlite-planner/src/decision_contract.rs
- Two AccessPath fixtures in the test module gain `probe: None` to
  keep mod tests compiling after the shape change — matches the
  existing pattern elsewhere in the planner.

crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs
- IndexScanEquality directive construction:
  Previous: `index_key_is_expression.then(|| … expression-index
  lookup …).flatten()` — fell back to None for the
  "column is a plain column" case, forcing the caller to re-derive
  the target expression later.
  New: split the two cases explicitly. Expression indexes still
  call the planner_directive helper; plain-column indexes now read
  `access_path.probe` and pull the Equality target out of it with
  a match. Eliminates one WHERE-term rewalk per dispatched query.
- IndexScanRange directive: same split. Expression path unchanged
  and will early-return None if it can't resolve; column-index
  path reads `Range { lower, upper }` from the probe and
  reconstructs `PlannerIndexRangeBound { expr, inclusive }` for
  each side.
- Both new branches clone the boxed Expr via
  `e.as_ref().clone()` — the probe is borrowed, the directive
  owns its copy.

Net effect: the planner writes probes once; the connection reads
them. No duplicate WHERE-term walking; cheaper to add further
directives that need the same data.
